title: A New Blueprint for Return: ACC Rolls Out Phased 'Home Station' Reintegration Strategy
summary: Recognizing that the return from a deployment is just the beginning of a new mission, Air Combat Command is introducing a comprehensive framework to strengthen home station reintegration.

title: AFW2 helps Davis-Monthan chief redefine recovery through Warrior Games
summary: Chief Master Sgt. Danielle Spurlock competed in the Department of Defense Warrior Games after being diagnosed with a rare cancer.

title: Davis-Monthan Hosts Final A-10 Range Day
summary: The desert air carried something heavier than heat on the morning of June 25, 2026. As the A-10C Thunderbolt II made one of its final passes over the Barry M. title: Monsoon Safety
summary: Airmen at Davis-Monthan Air Force Base are encouraged to remain weather-aware and prepared as Arizona’s monsoon season brings rapidly changing and potentially hazardous conditions.

title: Davis-Monthan hosts PJ Immersion Day for Special Warfare candidates
summary: Air Force Special Warfare recruiters partnered with the 306th Rescue Squadron to host a Pararescue Immersion Day providing prospective candidates an opportunity to experience the demands and mission of the career field firsthand at Davis-Monthan Air Force Base, Arizona, June 15th, 2026.

title: DM Airmen partner to safely relocate an Africanized bee colony
summary: Airmen from the 355th Wing Safety Office, 355th Civil Engineer Squadron Pest Management and a volunteer beekeeper partnered to safely remove and relocate an Africanized bee colony discovered beneath a toddler playground June 15, 2026.

title: Nutrition and Hydration Critical to Airman Readiness in the Summer Heat
summary: As temperatures across the installation regularly exceed 100 degrees Fahrenheit during the summer months, health officials at Davis-Monthan are reinforcing a foundational aspect of Airman readiness: proper nutrition and hydration.

title: Rescue mission highlights readiness of 34 WPS, 355 AMXS
summary: In a remarkable display of rapid response and seamless coordination, Airmen from the 355th Aircraft Maintenance Squadron and the 34th Weapons Squadron teamed up to rescue an F-35 Lightning II pilot who ejected during a training mission near Creech Air Force Base, Nevada, March 31.

title: Freedom Fest 2026
summary: Fireworks burst over Freedom Fest at Davis-Monthan Air Force Base, Arizona, June 12, 2026. Freedom

title: Davis-Monthan fire personnel and lifeguards train for water emergencies
summary: Davis-Monthan Fire Department personnel and base lifeguards trained together at the base pool to strengthen water emergency response, improve coordination and encourage families to practice water safety before the summer swimming season.

title: Pedro 66 memorial honors fallen rescue Airmen, preserves legacy of sacrifice
summary: More than 300 Airmen, veterans and members of the rescue community gathered at Davis-Monthan Air Force Base, June 5, 2026 to honor the legacy of Pedro 66, a crew of rescue Airmen whose sacrifice continues to shape generations of combat rescue professionals.

title: Explosive Ordnance Disposal conducts field training exercise
summary: Physically demanding scenarios helped EOD Airmen build the endurance, teamwork, and decision-making skills necessary to execute missions and recover personnel under challenging conditions.

title: A-10 engine build marks closing chapter at Davis-Monthan
summary: Airmen at Davis-Monthan Air Force Base recently completed their final A-10 Thunderbolt II engine build, marking the end of a proud 50-year legacy of maintaining the iconic close-air-support aircraft as the base transitions to future missions.

title: Police Week Ruck
summary: Every year, Airmen across Davis-Monthan Air Force Base observe Police Week, a weeklong observance that pays tribute to the sacrifice, strength and unwavering commitment of Security Forces members and law enforcement personnel. title: D-M team competes in first-ever FM innovation contest finals
summary: Two airmen from Davis-Monthan Air Force Base are finalists in the inaugural Financial Management Innovation Rodeo for their "Lightning FM" platform, a digital tool designed to streamline and standardize the Air Force's purchase request process.

title: CBRNE Training Bolsters Readiness at Davis-Monthan during Mosaic Lightning 26-2
summary: U.S. Airmen across Davis-Monthan Air Force Base installation enhanced mission readiness through Chemical, Biological, Radiological, Nuclear, Explosives (CBRNE) response training during some of the most complex contingency scenarios featured in exercise Mosaic Lightning 26-2, May 7-15, 2026.

title: Mosaic Lightning 26-2
summary: Airmen with the 355th Wing tested their ability to deploy as a cohesive, self-sustaining combat force under the Air Expeditionary Wing 2.0 model during the Mosaic Lightning 26-2 exercise at Davis-Monthan Air Force Base, Arizona, May 7-15.

title: 355 SFS and 355 CES EOD flight respond to abandoned vehicle during ML 26-2
summary: Airmen assigned to the 355th Security Forces Squadron and 355 Civil Engineer Squadron explosive ordnance disposal flight respond to a reported abandoned vehicle during Mosaic Lightning 26-2 at Davis-Monthan Air Force Base, Arizona, May 8, 2026.

title: 355th Maintenance Group Change of Command Ceremony
summary: 355th Wing leadership attend the 355th Maintenance Group change of command ceremony at Davis-Monthan Air Force Base, Arizona, May 8, 2026. Change of command ceremonies symbolize the formal transfer of authority, responsibility and accountability from one commander to another.

title: Dragons in the Dirt: 357th Fighter Squadron Unleashes Expeditionary A-10 Lethality
summary: From the high-desert heat of Twentynine Palms to the maritime environment of San Clemente Island, the “Dragons” of the 357th Fighter Squadron recently pushed the A-10C Thunderbolt II aircraft to its limits during a high-intensity, two-week temporary duty (TDY) assignment.

title: CBRN sUAS exercise kicks off Mosaic Lightning 26-2
summary: 355th Wing Drone Innovation Cell Airmen conducted a small unmanned aircraft system exercise that simulated a chemical, biological, radiological and nuclear (CBRN) attack on a military installation during Mosaic Lightning 26-2 at Davis-Monthan Air Force Base, May 8, 2026.

title: Back from the Boneyard: Tinker Brings B-1 Back to the Fight
summary: A U.S. Air Force B-1B Lancer once parked in the Arizona desert is back in the air after an intensive regeneration and depot maintenance effort led by the Oklahoma City Air Logistics Complex at Tinker Air Force Base.

title: Davis-Monthan Air Force Base Airmen participate in base’s first Norwegian Foot March
summary: More than 100 U.S. Airmen and Soldiers participated in the 355th Wing’s first Norwegian Foot March at Davis-Monthan Air Force Base, Arizona, May 2, 2026.

title: DMAFB and AFAS leadership collaborate to help Airmen
summary: Retired U.S. Air Force Maj. Gen. Ed Thomas, Air and Space Forces Aid Society chief executive officer, and Packy Lyden, AFAS chief development officer, met with Davis-Monthan Air Force Base installation leaders to discuss the organization’s impact April 29, 2026.

title: 25th OWS Annual Nylander Day memorial ceremony 2026
summary: U.S. Airmen assigned to the 25th Operational Weather Squadron gathered Friday, April 24, 2026 to honor U.S. Air Force Capt. Nathan J. Nylander, a weather officer who made the ultimate sacrifice April 27, 2011, during an enemy attack in Kabul, Afghanistan.

title: Three Davis-Monthan units recognized for unprecedented rescue efforts during final withdrawal of U.S. troops from Afghanistan
summary: After distinguishing themselves during a deployment to Afghanistan that included the final withdrawal of U.S. troops from the country in 2021, two units, the 55th and 48th Rescue Squadrons, received the Presidential Unit Citation award at a ceremony at Davis-Monthan Air Force Base, Arizona, April 17

title: New Human Performance Optimization facility to enhance readiness at DMAFB
summary: Airmen and leadership gathered for a groundbreaking ceremony marking the start of construction on a new Human Performance Optimization facility. title: Space Forces - Southern completes first Human Performance Assessment at DM
summary: Guardians assigned to Space Forces - Southern completed their first-ever Human Performance Assessment on April 23, 2026, marking a historic milestone as the unit conducted an evaluation for the first time since its activation and a historical first for first at Davis-Monthan Air Force Base.

title: Air Force Special Warfare employs Kinetic Interceptor in counter-UAS proof of concept
summary: Air Force Special Warfare Airmen from the 48th Rescue Squadron, 7th Air Support Operations Squadron, and 316th Civil Engineer Squadron Explosive Ordnance Disposal conducted a proof-of-concept event on April 7, 2026. The event integrated a commercial off-the-shelf kinetic interceptor with an expediti

title: 68th Rescue Squadron completes annual Combat Leader Course
summary: U.S. Air Force Pararescuemen assigned to the 68th Rescue Squadron completed the Combat Leader Course at Davis-Monthan Air Force Base, Arizona, April 17, 2026, enhancing their operational readiness and leadership skills through a series of simulated mission objectives to test both their tactical prof

title: Combat Leader Course Triage

title: 11th ATF Inactivation Ceremony
summary: The 11th Air Task Force was officially inactivated during a ceremony at Davis-Monthan Air Force Base, Arizona, April 17, 2026, marking the end of the unit’s mission after nearly two years of operations and a historic first deployment.

title: DM Command Team Takes the PT Test
summary: Airmen assigned to the 355th Wing participated in a diagnostic physical training test alongside wing leadership to evaluate recently updated fitness standards at Davis-Monthan Air Force Base, April 15, 2026.

title: DM Command Team Leads the Way Taking New PFRA
summary: 355th Wing leadership led from the front as they completed a diagnostic Physical Fitness Risk Assessment (PFRA) alongside Airmen, setting the standard for implementation of the Department of the Air Force’s updated fitness program April 15, 2026.

title: DM50 Picnic and Car Show
summary: The DM50 picnic and car show brought together service members, their families and the Tucson community for a day of connection April 11, 2026.

title: That Others May Live: The unwavering commitment of Air Force combat search and rescue
summary:
In the U.S. Air Force, no phrase carries more weight than "That Others May Live." It is not merely a motto; it is a commitment that underpins the existence of the Combat Search and Rescue (CSAR) mission.

summary: DAVIS-MONTHAN AIR FORCE BASE, Ariz. – F-16 aircraft and personnel assigned to Holloman Air Force Base, New Mexico will be training out of Davis-Monthan Air Force Base, Arizona from April 10 to May 15.

title: New Mission Ready Airmen program strengthens deployment readiness
summary: DAVIS-MONTHAN AIR FORCE BASE, Ariz. — U.S. Airmen at Davis-Monthan Air Force Base are expanding their deployment readiness through the Mission Ready Airmen program, an initiative designed to prepare service members for the challenges they may face in deployed environments.

title: 355th Wing commanders explore leadership lessons during offsite In Gardner Canyon
summary: DAVIS-MONTHAN AIR FORCE BASE, Ariz. -- Leaders from across Davis-Monthan Air Force Base’s 355th Wing gathered for an offsite focused on strengthening relationships, building morale and preparing for future challenges in Gardner Canyon, Arizona, March 23-24, 2026.

title: DM hosts a Senior Selects Professional Enhancement Seminar
summary: The 355th Wing hosted a Professional Enhancement Seminar to prepare newly selected senior master sergeants for the transition into one of the Air Force’s most demanding leadership roles March 25–27, 2026.

title: A-10 Makes Final Luke Days Appearance
summary: A-10C Thunderbolt II aircraft from Davis-Monthan Air Force Base performed a combat arms demonstration at the Luke Days Air Show at Luke AFB, Arizona, March 22, 2026–marking their final Luke Days appearance as the aircraft divests and pilots transition to the F-35A Lightning II and other Air Force as
